_comment: Declared content-placement intents — the write side of 'declare where a page goes, let a build script place it', extending the existing vex:department/vex:workType meta-tag pattern to arc membership. lib/apply-content-intents.js reads this file, applies each intent with status:"pending" (upserts vex:department/vex:workType meta tags on the target pages/{slug}.html, and — if arcKey is set — adds the slug to that arc's auto-managed 'Auto-Placed' section in data/arcs-v2.json, first removing it from any other arc's auto-managed section so a re-placed slug never ends up double-listed), then marks it status:"applied" with an appliedAt timestamp. An intent never writes into a hand-curated explicit section — arc-insertion here only ever creates or edits the one auto-managed section per arc; moving a slug into permanent hand-curated position stays a human curatorial act, same as the department/workType default already works. Each intent's department must already be registered in data/departments.json and its arcKey (if set) must be a real arc — lib/apply-content-intents.js's validateIntent() rejects and reports anything else rather than silently producing content that exists in data/index.json but renders nowhere. See docs/architecture/03-data.md's 'Content-placement intents' section.
